creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Jo, good day.

Thu, Jan 03, 2008 at 11:58:15AM -0800, Jo Rhett wrote:
> Eygene, may I ask why you want FreeBSD to patch something against the 
> wishes of the code maintainer? cfengine 2.x requires BDB 4.4 or greater.

May I first note that the port maintainer said 'USE_BDB?=   44',
so this can be overriden by the administrator.  I decided to use
4.3 at the time of the PR submission, because the system used BDB
4.3 everywhere and cfengine works with that version just fine.

There is no strict technical reasons why not to use 4.4, but I found
no reasons not to use the older BDB packages.

[Speaking about the changes in instrument.c and cfshow.c]

The CFEngine manual at http://www.cfengine.org/docs/cfengine-Reference.html
says
-----
   In order to install cfengine, you should first ensure that the following
   packages are installed.

   OpenSSL
           Open source Secure Sockets Layer for encryption.
           URL: http://www.openssl.org

   BerkeleyDB (version 3.2 or later)
           Light-weight flat-file database system.
           URL: http://www.sleepycat.com
-----
So BDB >= 3.2 will go, if the manual is not very outdated.  Moreover,
Mark Burgess accepted those patches, so I believe that they are correct.

[Speaking about patches to configure]

If you will read my follow-up from 3rd of August 2007, the port
does not honour the Berkeley DB version I requested via WITH_BDB_VER
and it linked the port against 4.0.

> And this change would not be FreeBSD specific, so should probably be taken 
> up with Mark instead.

Yes, the changes were accepted to the mainstream CFEngine.  But at
the time of submission there were no releases with that patches,
so the best I can do is to submit the patches for the FreeBSD port
to make it work 'just now' for other people, not just for me.
-- 
Eygene
